data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

The latest announcement is out from China First Capital Group Ltd. ( (HK:1269) ).

China First Capital Group Ltd. has provided an update on ongoing legal proceedings concerning a new winding-up petition filed against the company in Hong Kong. The High Court has adjourned the hearing of both the substitution application and the new petition from 20 April 2026 to 20 July 2026.

The company said it will continue to seek legal advice as the case progresses and pledged to issue further announcements on any significant developments. It also urged shareholders and potential investors to exercise caution when dealing in its shares, underscoring the continuing uncertainty surrounding the winding-up proceedings.

More about China First Capital Group Ltd.

China First Capital Group Ltd., incorporated in the Cayman Islands and listed in Hong Kong, operates as a financial services group. The company focuses on capital markets and related investment activities, serving shareholders and investors in Hong Kong and broader regional markets.

data.title: Midland Holdings Strengthens Board with Appointment of Veteran Independent Director
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Midland Holdings Limited ( (HK:1200) ) has provided an announcement.

Midland Holdings Limited has appointed veteran finance professional Mr. Li Wai Keung as an independent non-executive director, as well as a member of its audit, remuneration, and nomination committees, effective 20 April 2026. With more than 47 years of financial management experience and multiple past and current roles at major Hong Kong-listed companies, Li is expected to strengthen the group’s corporate governance and oversight, although his concurrent directorship at China South City, which is under a court-ordered winding up, may draw added investor scrutiny to his governance track record.

The board confirmed that Li meets all independence criteria under Hong Kong listing rules, holds no shares in Midland and has no relationships with existing directors, senior management, or major shareholders. His 18‑month term, subject to rotation and re-election, includes an annual director’s fee of HK$260,000, aligning his remuneration with market practice and reflecting the board’s view of his experience, time commitment, and responsibilities across key board committees.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:1200) stock is a Buy with a HK$3.63 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Midland Holdings Limited stock, see the HK:1200 Stock Forecast page.

More about Midland Holdings Limited

Midland Holdings Limited is a Hong Kong-based group primarily engaged in real estate agency services, focusing on property brokerage and related professional services in the local market. The company operates through various subsidiaries to serve residential, commercial, and industrial property segments, positioning itself as a key intermediary in Hong Kong’s property sector.

Midland Holdings Limited ( (HK:1200) ) has provided an announcement.

Midland Holdings Limited has announced an updated composition of its board of directors effective 20 April 2026, confirming Freddie Wong as chairman, Angela Wong as deputy chairman and managing director, and Sze Ka Ming as chief financial officer, alongside four independent non-executive directors. The company has also refreshed the membership of its audit, remuneration, nomination, and risk committees, a move that clarifies governance roles and may strengthen oversight, risk management, and transparency for shareholders and other stakeholders.

The new structure assigns key committee chairmanships to independent non-executive directors, while executive directors hold defined positions across the remuneration, nomination, and risk committees. This governance configuration underscores Midland’s effort to balance executive influence with independent oversight, which can enhance board effectiveness, regulatory compliance, and investor confidence in the company’s decision-making processes.

data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

Industrial and Commercial Bank of China ( (HK:1398) ) has issued an announcement.

Industrial and Commercial Bank of China Limited has completed the issuance of its 2026 Undated Additional Tier 1 Capital Bonds (Series 1) via Bond Connect in China’s National Interbank Bond Market. The offering, approved by relevant regulators, underscores the bank’s active use of capital markets to support its balance sheet and regulatory capital structure.

The bonds, which were book-run on 16 April 2026 and settled on 20 April 2026, raised RMB30 billion at a 2.01% distribution rate for the first five years, subject to rate adjustment every five years thereafter. Proceeds will be used to replenish the bank’s additional Tier 1 capital, enhancing its loss-absorbing capacity and capital adequacy, while the issuer retains conditional redemption rights from the fifth anniversary on each distribution payment date.

data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

China Internet Investment Finance Holdings Ltd. ( (HK:0810) ) has shared an update.

China Castson 81 Finance Company Limited has completed a rights issue on the basis of five rights shares for every two existing shares at HK$0.102 per share, raising net proceeds of about HK$49.24 million. Although only around 53.7% of the rights shares were taken up by shareholders and no unsubscribed shares were placed in the market, the issue became unconditional after the underwriter, Goodchamp, subscribed for all remaining 235,844,989 untaken rights shares, consolidating its stake and ensuring the capital raise went ahead as planned.

The company plans to allocate roughly 70% of the funds, or about HK$34.47 million, to investments in listed and unlisted securities, including equity in companies engaged in photovoltaics, renewable energy, hydrogen energy, energy storage, artificial intelligence and digital assets, by the end of 2026. The remaining 30%, or about HK$14.77 million, will be used for general working capital needs such as staff costs, professional fees, administrative expenses and other operating costs through March 2027, supporting both its expansion into high-growth sectors and the stability of its ongoing operations.

More about China Internet Investment Finance Holdings Ltd.

China Castson 81 Finance Company Limited is a Bermuda-incorporated investment and finance company listed in Hong Kong. It focuses on deploying capital into listed and unlisted securities, targeting sectors such as new energy solutions, artificial intelligence and digital assets, while also supporting its broader financial services and investment operations with working capital.

data.title: Travel Expert Reports Cyber Intrusion but Says Operations Remain Stable
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

The latest update is out from Travel Expert (Asia) Enterprises Limited ( (HK:1235) ).

Travel Expert (Asia) Enterprises Limited has disclosed that it recently detected unauthorized access to certain internal servers and shared storage systems, potentially affecting customer orders, operational records, employee files and system data, though credit card details are not at risk as payment processing is handled by external providers. The group has launched emergency measures including forming an internal crisis committee, engaging independent cybersecurity experts for forensic investigation and data recovery, notifying law enforcement and Hong Kong’s privacy regulator, and activating its business continuity plan using cloud-based tools, with management stating that overall operations remain stable while it assesses process-level impacts and commits to strengthening information system security.

The board will closely monitor developments and provide further updates in line with Hong Kong listing and securities regulations, including any changes in customer data security risks. Shareholders and potential investors are urged to act cautiously as current disclosures are based on preliminary internal assessments and the full extent of the incident’s consequences for internal processes and compliance obligations is still under review.

More about Travel Expert (Asia) Enterprises Limited

Travel Expert (Asia) Enterprises Limited is a Hong Kong-listed travel services group that provides travel-related products and services to customers across Asia. The company focuses on handling customer orders and related operational records, and relies on third-party processors and banks to manage its credit card-related transactions, reflecting an asset-light approach to payment processing and data security in that area.

Yankuang Energy Group has disclosed first-quarter 2026 operating data from its controlled subsidiary Yancoal Australia, highlighting a softer performance in key coal metrics. data.title: Yankuang Energy Posts Mixed Q1 2026 Volumes as It Refines Coal Chemicals Portfolio
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

The latest announcement is out from Yankuang Energy Group Company Limited Class H ( (HK:1171) ).

Yankuang Energy reported mixed operational metrics for the first quarter of 2026, with saleable coal production declining 6.28% year on year even as total coal sales rose 3.07%, driven by a modest increase in self-produced coal sales. The coal chemicals segment generally showed higher output and sales for key products like methanol, acetic acid, caprolactam, polyformaldehyde, liquid paraffin, and naphtha, while ethyl acetate and urea recorded notable volume drops as the company adjusted its product mix in response to changing market conditions, underscoring a strategic shift toward optimizing profitability amid volatile demand.

The sharp reduction in ethyl acetate production and sales stemmed from flexible production management at subsidiary Yanzhou Lunan Chemical, indicating a deliberate rebalancing of the portfolio rather than purely demand-driven weakness. Overall, the data highlight Yankuang Energy’s efforts to stabilize coal sales despite lower output and to refine its chemicals slate, moves that could influence revenue composition and margin performance as it adapts to macroeconomic and market fluctuations.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:1171) stock is a Hold with a HK$9.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Yankuang Energy Group Company Limited Class H stock, see the HK:1171 Stock Forecast page.

More about Yankuang Energy Group Company Limited Class H

Yankuang Energy Group Company Limited is a China-based integrated energy company focused on coal production and coal chemicals. The group operates extensive coal mining assets and downstream chemical facilities, supplying coal and value-added chemical products such as methanol, glycol, acetic acid, and other derivatives to domestic and international markets.

Town Ray Holdings Limited ( (HK:1692) ) has provided an announcement.

Town Ray Holdings Limited has called its annual general meeting for 27 May 2026 in Hong Kong, where shareholders will review the audited consolidated financial statements and directors’ and auditor’s reports for the year ended 31 December 2025. The agenda underscores the company’s adherence to corporate governance norms, including renewal of its external auditor, board changes and director remuneration.

Shareholders will vote on a proposed final dividend of HK7.5 cents per share for 2025, re-appointment of Ernst & Young as auditor, re-election of existing directors and election of several new executive directors, as well as a general mandate for the board to issue new shares and related securities. The outcomes will shape Town Ray’s capital management flexibility, board composition and cash returns to investors for the coming year.

More about Town Ray Holdings Limited

data.description:

The latest update is out from Miramar Hotel & Investment Co ( (HK:0071) ).

Miramar Hotel and Investment Company, Limited plans to adopt new articles of association to align its corporate governance framework with recent changes to Hong Kong’s Companies Ordinance and the Stock Exchange’s Listing Rules. The move reflects the company’s adaptation to the treasury share regime and modern communication practices, including website-based dissemination of corporate communications.

The proposed changes will also support the expansion of a paperless listing environment by enabling hybrid and virtual general meetings, electronic voting, e-submission of proxies and notices, and electronic payment of corporate action proceeds. In addition, the company intends to streamline general meeting procedures, improve operational efficiency, and remove the 5% material interest voting threshold for directors, subject to shareholder approval at the annual general meeting on 8 June 2026.

More about Miramar Hotel & Investment Co

Miramar Hotel and Investment Company, Limited is a Hong Kong-incorporated company engaged in the hotel and investment sector. The company is listed on the Main Board of the Stock Exchange of Hong Kong under stock code 71, serving a broad base of shareholders in the local capital market.
